Column.description: Flex column container that stacks children vertically. Use for page layout, form sections, and any vertical arrangement of components.

children.description: Ordered list of child component IDs to render in this column

Row.description: Flex row container that arranges children horizontally. Use for side-by-side layouts, icon+text pairs, and toolbar-style arrangements.

children.description: Ordered list of child component IDs to render in this row

Divider.description: Visual separator line. Use to divide sections within a page.

Text.description: Text display component. Supports headings, body copy, and captions. Use 'variant' to control visual hierarchy.

text.description: Text content to display, or a data binding path (e.g. {"path": "/user/name"})

variant.description: Visual style variant. h1/h2/h3 for headings, body for standard text, caption for small secondary text

Icon.description: Renders a Material Symbols icon by name. Use for visual affordance alongside text or as standalone indicators.

Image.description: Image display component. Renders an image from a URL with configurable object-fit behavior.

url.description: Fully qualified URL of the image to display

fit.description: CSS object-fit behavior: 'cover' crops to fill space, 'contain' letterboxes, 'fill' stretches

GlassButton.description: Tappable button with multiple style variants. Triggers server-side actions or client-side navigation. Use 'filled' for primary actions, 'outlined' for secondary, 'text' for tertiary.

children.description: Child component IDs rendered inside the button (typically a Text or Icon)

variant.description: Visual style: 'filled' = solid background (primary CTA), 'outlined' = border only, 'text' = no border

action.description: Action to perform on tap. Use {name: string} for server events or {functionCall: {call: string, args: object}} for client-side navigation.

name.description: Server event name. The agent receives this event name when the button is tapped.

args.description: Arguments to pass to the function

GlassNavigator.description: Invisible tappable wrapper that makes any child component clickable. Use to make images, cards, or layout regions trigger navigation or actions.

child.description: ID of the single child component to wrap

target.description: Shorthand for navigation — surface ID to navigate to on tap

action.description: Action to perform on tap. Use {name: string} for server events or {functionCall: {call: string, args: object}} for client-side navigation.

name.description: Server event name sent when the child is tapped

GlassCarousel.description: Swipeable multi-page container with dot indicators. Use to present multiple screens or steps in a single mini-app. Each child is one full page.

children.description: Ordered list of child component IDs, one per carousel page

GlassHeader.description: Prominent page header with a title and optional subtitle. Typically placed at the top of a surface to give the page context.

subtitle.description: Secondary text displayed below the title

children.description: Optional action button component IDs (rendered right-aligned)

GlassListItem.description: List row component with leading avatar or icon, primary and secondary text, and optional trailing content. Use for contact lists, settings rows, feed items, and any repeated row patterns.

primary.description: Main label text for the list row

secondary.description: Smaller supporting text displayed below the primary label

icon.description: Material Symbols icon name shown as the leading element (use instead of 'avatar')

avatar.description: URL of an image displayed as a circular avatar (use instead of 'icon')

trailing.description: Short text shown at the trailing edge of the row (e.g. a timestamp, count, or status)

initials.description: Fallback initials when no icon or avatar is set

badgeVariant.description: Color variant for the trailing badge

chevron.description: Show a right-pointing chevron indicating tappable

action.description: Action to trigger when the row is tapped.

GlassBadge.description: Compact status indicator pill. Use inline with other components to show status, severity, or categorization at a glance.

text.description: Short label text to display inside the badge (e.g. 'Active', 'Failed', 'New')
